# Strategy Evaluation Reference

---

## 1. Return Metrics — 5/10/15-Year Averages

The three core return metrics reveal how efficiently management deploys capital. Calculate averages over 5, 10, and 15 years where data is available.

### Return on Capital Employed (ROCE)

**Formula:** EBIT / (Total Assets − Current Liabilities)

| ROCE Level | Interpretation |
|------------|---------------|
| > 20% | Exceptional — pricing power or capital-light model |
| 15–20% | Strong — above cost of capital |
| 10–15% | Adequate |
| < 10% | Weak — may be destroying value |

### Return on Invested Capital (ROIC)

**Formula:** NOPAT / (Total Equity + Total Debt − Cash)

Where NOPAT = EBIT × (1 − Tax Rate)

- **ROIC > WACC** = value creation (spread is the key metric)
- **ROIC < WACC** = value destruction regardless of growth

Benchmark against industry peers; capital-heavy industries (utilities, mining) structurally have lower ROIC.

### Return on Equity (ROE)

**Formula:** Net Income / Average Shareholders' Equity

- ROE inflated by high leverage is misleading — always cross-check with ROIC
- Consistent ROE > 15% without excessive debt = quality signal

### Scoring Guide — Return Metrics

| Score | Criteria |
|-------|----------|
| 5 | ROIC > 20% sustained; ROCE expanding; ROE > 18% without heavy leverage |
| 4 | ROIC 15–20%; consistent ROCE; ROE 13–18% |
| 3 | ROIC 10–15%; stable returns; ROE 8–13% |
| 2 | ROIC 5–10%; declining trend; leveraged ROE |
| 1 | ROIC < 5% or declining; ROE driven by debt; ROIC < WACC |

---

## 2. Capital Allocation

Map where the company deploys its FCF. Look at the last 5 years of cash flow statements and ask:

### Where is cash going?

| Destination | Positive Signal | Concern |
|-------------|----------------|---------|
| R&D investment | Growing R&D as % of revenue in a tech/pharma company | Cutting R&D to prop up margins |
| Organic capex | Investing in capacity for future growth | Maintenance-only capex = low-growth mode |
| Debt repayment | Deleveraging from past acquisitions | Slow repayment when leverage is high |
| Dividends | Disciplined, growing dividend | Paying dividends while FCF negative |
| Share buybacks | Buying back at below intrinsic value | Buying back at peak valuations |
| Acquisitions | Bolt-ons with clear strategic fit | Serial acquirers with poor integration track record |

### Capital Allocation Quality Score

- 5 — Management articulates clear capital hierarchy; historically high-return investments; buybacks at sensible valuations
- 4 — Good track record; minor missteps (e.g., one dilutive acquisition)
- 3 — Adequate; no clear strategy communicated; mixed M&A outcomes
- 2 — Poor capital discipline; acquisitions at peak multiples; dividends funded by debt
- 1 — Destructive allocation; persistent dilution; value-destroying M&A; no buyback discipline

---

## 3. Competitive Moat Assessment

Look for structural advantages that protect returns over time:

| Moat Type | Evidence to Look For |
|-----------|---------------------|
| Pricing Power | Ability to raise prices above inflation without losing volume |
| Switching Costs | Customer retention > 90%; long contract durations; deep integration |
| Network Effects | Value grows as user base grows (e.g. payments, marketplaces, social) |
| Cost Advantage | Scale economies, proprietary process, or structural low-cost position |
| Intangible Assets | Patents, brands, licences, regulatory approvals |
| Efficient Scale | Natural monopoly or duopoly in a niche market |

**Scoring Guide — Moat:**
- 5 — Multiple reinforcing moat sources; pricing power demonstrated; retention > 95%
- 4 — Clear single moat; high retention; some pricing ability
- 3 — Narrow moat; moderate retention; price-taker in some segments
- 2 — Weak or eroding moat; competitive pressure intensifying
- 1 — No identifiable moat; commodity product; race to the bottom on price

---

## 4. Customer Satisfaction & Brand Sentiment

Cross-reference quantitative financials with qualitative customer signals:

| Source | What to Look For |
|--------|-----------------|
| Trustpilot / Google Reviews | Overall score trend; volume of reviews; response quality |
| App Store / Play Store | Rating trend over last 12 months; recent review themes |
| Reddit (r/investing, r/<company>) | Community sentiment; recurring complaints |
| NPS data | Disclosed in earnings / investor presentations or third-party surveys |
| Glassdoor | Employee satisfaction trend (proxy for culture and management quality) |
| Social media & news | Brand perception, PR crises, viral customer issues |

**Scoring Guide — Customer & Brand:**
- 5 — Strong, improving NPS; high app ratings (>4.3); positive community sentiment
- 4 — Good ratings; minor complaints; responsive to feedback
- 3 — Average ratings; some recurring complaints; neutral sentiment
- 2 — Below-average ratings; persistent product/service complaints; declining trend
- 1 — Significant brand damage; regulatory complaints; customer exodus reported

---

## Combined Strategy Score

Average the sub-scores across Return Metrics, Capital Allocation, Competitive Moat, and Customer Satisfaction to arrive at the single **Strategy & Returns score out of 5** reported in the main assessment.
